Selera Hijau Berhad (Company No. 202001012345), a Main Market issuer on Bursa Malaysia, reports an operational cyber event on 02 Jun 2026; no service interruption to disclosure systems occurred.
Event summary: credential stuffing attempts against vendor-facing SSO were detected and blocked by MFA; SIEM shows no lateral movement and no export of MNPI.
Investigation scope covered the finance shared drive, HR onboarding forms, and DPA Schedule 1–3 with Ternakan Cloud Sdn Bhd (processor) and LapisPay Services Bhd (sub-processor, sandbox only).
We confirm no access to material non-public information; references to the discontinued Project Hutan were already disclosed in a Bursa Malaysia announcement (Ref: BM/ANN/2025-1188).
Privacy handling status: prior DSARs dated 10 May 2026 and 18 May 2026 were completed with erasure letters issued; no new DSAR was received in connection with this event.
Consent management: an employee withdrew consent for optional wellness-data processing on 15 May 2026 and the attribute was flagged “Do Not Collect”; logs show the minimisation rule was applied.
Cross-border transfer controls: Data Export Approval DEX/2026/014 remains in force under PDPA s.129 and DPA Schedule 2; no export was triggered by this incident.
Retention and deletion: per Policy DP-07, applicant CVs are retained 24 months; the March 2026 purge completed and certificates of erasure were archived.
Positive finding: a contractor onboarding sheet containing names and office contact fields was briefly synced to a non-MY staging bucket for 46 minutes before auto-recall; hashing was not applied at sync time.
Remediation: the staging rule now enforces field-level hashing and regional pinning; the affected file was replaced with a minimised version and notice sent to the contractor firm’s role mailbox.
ESG and operations: no health, safety, or environmental impacts; supply chain and payroll runs proceeded; the vendor declared no sub-processor change under DPA Schedule 4.
MNPI assessment under CMSA: no unpublished price-sensitive data was involved; finance figures referenced were quarter-end numbers already lodged via Bursa’s e-submission.
Financing arrangements: facility agreements with Nusacapital Bank Berhad carry confidentiality undertakings; no facility schedules were accessed and no negative pledge information was in scope.
Employment and onboarding forms remain template-only in production; fields labelled “NRIC/Passport” and “Bank Account No.” are placeholders and were not populated in the affected file.
Reporting and contacts: internal incident reference SHB/IR/2026-06-02; PDPA enquiries via privacy@selerahijau.example and Bursa-related queries via ir@selerahijau.example.
Negative control affirmation: there is no ongoing consent withdrawal backlog, no retention expiry missed, and no live erasure request pending.
Governance: the Audit Committee reviewed the incident and closed the item; no Bursa immediate announcement is required under the Listing Requirements.
